message:
feat(core,cli,block): include DIR_INDEX items in btrfs dir dump, align IoEngine stats with submission semantics

Widen walk_btrfs_dir_items -> walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items to return both
DIR_ITEM and DIR_INDEX payloads with their item type, so the CLI hex
dump now surfaces dir_index entries alongside dir_item ones (closes
bd-5yuh). The DumpHexBlockOutput struct gains an optional item_kind
field for btrfs entries.

In ffs-block IoEngine, move read/write op-count increments before the
syscall so stats reflect submitted ops rather than only successful ones
(tracked as bd-iu2z). Add failed-read-counts-submission test for both
MemIoEngine and PreadPwriteEngine, and update proptest assertions to
match the new counting semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-5s0r.4","title":"E2E test: write-back cache durability and flush correctness","description":"# E2E test: write-back cache durability and flush correctness

## GOAL
Prove that the write-back cache correctly flushes all committed data to disk, never loses dirty blocks, and correctly handles abort/commit lifecycle with MVCC integration.

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Basic Flush Correctness
1. Mount FrankenFS with write-back cache enabled (5s flush interval)
2. Write 1000 blocks with known content
3. Wait for 2 flush intervals (10s)
4. Unmount cleanly
5. Remount, read all 1000 blocks
6. Verify: all blocks contain correct data (BLAKE3 checksums match)

### Scenario 2: Clean Unmount Flushes Everything
1. Write 500 blocks, do NOT wait for flush interval
2. Immediately unmount (clean shutdown)
3. Verify: unmount blocked until all dirty blocks flushed
4. Remount, verify all 500 blocks present and correct

### Scenario 3: SIGKILL Dirty Block Loss
1. Write 100 blocks with fsync (durable baseline)
2. Write 200 more blocks WITHOUT fsync
3. SIGKILL the FUSE process
4. Remount with crash recovery
5. Verify: 100 fsync blocks intact, 200 non-fsync blocks may be missing
6. Verify: no partial/corrupt blocks (either present or absent)

### Scenario 4: Abort Discards Dirty Blocks
1. Begin MVCC transaction T1
2. Write 50 blocks via T1
3. Abort T1
4. Verify: dirty blocks from T1 discarded (not flushed to disk)
5. Previous committed data for those blocks unchanged

### Scenario 5: Backpressure Under Load
1. Configure critical_watermark at 95% dirty ratio
2. Flood writes faster than flush can keep up
3. Verify: writes block when dirty ratio exceeds critical threshold
4. Verify: writes resume after flush daemon catches up
5. Verify: no OOM from unbounded dirty accumulation

### Scenario 6: Concurrent Transactions and Flush
1. 4 concurrent transactions, each writing 100 blocks
2. 2 transactions commit, 2 abort
3. Flush daemon running continuously
4. Verify: only committed transaction blocks persisted to disk
5. Verify: aborted transaction blocks not on disk

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 6 scenarios pass
- [ ] No data loss for committed + fsynced data under any scenario
- [ ] Abort correctly discards dirty blocks
- [ ] Backpressure prevents OOM
- [ ] SIGKILL scenario: no corrupt data (only missing unflushed data)
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for flush cycles, dirty ratios, backpressure events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenLark","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:33.610842191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:24:58.822810931Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:24:58.822791485Z","close_reason":"implemented e2e scenarios + full gates pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","e2e","test","writeback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.4","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:33.610842191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.4","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:33.610842191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.4","author":"GoldenLark","text":"Implemented deterministic write-back durability E2E coverage. Added:\n- crates/ffs-block/tests/writeback_e2e.rs (6 scenarios)\n- scripts/e2e/ffs_writeback_e2e.sh (shell runner integrated with existing e2e lib)\n- scripts/e2e/README.md usage/docs update\n- crates/ffs-block/Cargo.toml dev-dep: blake3\n\nScenario coverage:\n1) Basic Flush Correctness: 1000 block writes, daemon flush, remount+checksum verification\n2) Clean Unmount Flushes Everything: writes then daemon shutdown flush-all before remount verify\n3) SIGKILL Dirty Block Loss: fsync baseline preserved, non-fsync blocks absent-or-complete (no partial garbage)\n4) Abort Discards Dirty Blocks: staged txn writes aborted and never persisted\n5) Backpressure Under Load: sustained writes with daemon; bounded dirty growth and progress\n6) Concurrent Transactions + Flush: 4 txns (2 commit/2 abort), only committed ranges persisted\n\nValidation run:\n- PASS cargo fmt --check\n- PASS cargo check --all-targets\n- PASS cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings\n- PASS cargo test --workspace\n- PASS bash scripts/e2e/ffs_writeback_e2e.sh\n","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:24:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5s0r.5","title":"Cross-crate integration: write-back cache triggers RaptorQ symbol refresh","description":"# Cross-crate integration: write-back cache triggers RaptorQ symbol refresh

## GOAL
Wire the write-back cache flush path to trigger RaptorQ symbol refresh for modified block groups. This is the critical cross-crate integration between ffs-block (cache), ffs-repair (RaptorQ), and the flush daemon.

## BACKGROUND
Currently bd-820 (RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush) exists but is not wired into the write-back cache flush path. When FlushDaemon (bd-5s0r.2) flushes dirty blocks, it must notify the repair subsystem that those block groups need symbol recalculation.

## DELIVERABLES
1. FlushDaemon::flush_batch() calls repair::mark_groups_dirty(groups) after successful disk write
2. Repair subsystem queues symbol recalculation for dirty groups
3. Integration test: write -> flush -> verify symbols updated

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: flush_batch marks correct groups dirty
2. Unit: repair subsystem receives group notifications
3. Integration: full write -> flush -> symbol refresh -> corruption -> recovery cycle

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Flush triggers refresh (debug): group_ids, block_count
- Symbol refresh queued (debug): group_id, priority
- Symbol refresh complete (info): group_id, duration_ms, symbol_count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Every flushed block group eventually gets symbol refresh
2. No symbol staleness > 2 scrub intervals
3. Recovery works for blocks modified after last refresh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:19.841155673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T14:29:08.392646802Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T14:29:08.392627676Z","close_reason":"Completed: wired write-back flush to repair refresh queue; tests and workspace gates passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","repair","writeback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:19.841155673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:13.259910427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.5","depends_on_id":"bd-820","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:13.401810335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":121,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5s0r.6","title":"Add unit tests for write-back cache DirtyTracker and FlushDaemon","description":"# Add unit tests for write-back cache DirtyTracker and FlushDaemon

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Write-Back Cache epic (bd-5s0r). Currently has E2E test (bd-5s0r.4) but NO unit tests for DirtyTracker invariants or FlushDaemon behavior.

## TEST PLAN

### DirtyTracker Tests
1. Mark block dirty: dirty flag set, dirty count incremented
2. Mark block clean after flush: dirty flag cleared, count decremented
3. Dirty list ordering: oldest dirty block is first
4. Double-mark is idempotent
5. Mark clean on non-dirty block is no-op
6. Dirty ratio calculation is correct

### FlushDaemon Tests
7. Flush batch selects oldest dirty blocks first
8. Flush batch size respects configuration
9. Flush under backpressure: batch size reduced
10. Failed flush retries (block stays dirty)
11. fsync flushes all dirty blocks for specific inode
12. Daemon shutdown completes in-flight flush

### Eviction Coordination Tests
13. Dirty block cannot be evicted without flush
14. Flush failure prevents eviction (block stays in cache)
15. Clean block can be evicted immediately

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 15 tests above, deterministic, mock I/O layer.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full dirty tracker state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 15 tests pass
2. Dirty-block-never-evicted invariant verified
3. No flaky tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldenLark","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:50.138555772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:33.543548002Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:33.543526121Z","close_reason":"unit/integration coverage satisfied; tests green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","test","unit","writeback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:50.138555772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.200119190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5s0r.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.314066429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-5s0r.6","author":"GoldenLark","text":"Coverage now satisfies this bead via crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs unit tests plus new integration tests. Mapped coverage:\nDirtyTracker semantics: arc_cache_write_through_keeps_dirty_tracker_clean, arc_cache_dirty_blocks_order_oldest_first_and_rewrite_moves_to_tail, cache_metrics_initial_state, cache_metrics_track_hits_and_misses, cache_metrics_list_sizes.\nFlushDaemon semantics: flush_daemon_batch_flushes_oldest_first, flush_daemon_shutdown_flushes_all_dirty_blocks, flush_daemon_flushes_1000_blocks_within_two_intervals, arc_cache_write_back_critical_ratio_triggers_backpressure_flush.\nEviction invariants: arc_cache_explicit_evict_panics_for_dirty_block, arc_cache_explicit_evict_succeeds_for_clean_block, arc_cache_write_back_replacement_succeeds_after_critical_backpressure_flush.\nMVCC+flush lifecycle unit/integration: mvcc_uncommitted_dirty_blocks_are_not_flushed, mvcc_commit_then_flush_marks_persisted_with_pin, mvcc_concurrent_commit_abort_with_daemon_running, plus writeback_e2e scenario suite.\n\nValidation: cargo test -p ffs-block passes with all listed tests green; workspace test/clippy/check/fmt currently green in this snapshot.","created_at":"2026-02-13T20:25:30Z"}]}

- Cancelled errno mapping mismatch: COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC currently says EINTR; crates/ffs-error maps to ECANCELED.
- Parser error taxonomy needs explicit layering: ffs-ondisk returns ParseError today; user-facing layers return FfsError.
- PLAN references ARCHITECTURE.md and CONVENTIONS.md which do not exist; decide create vs remove.

Audit output should focus on: normative type/trait single-source-of-truth, crate map accuracy vs Cargo.toml, and eliminating phantom contracts/phantom crates.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:33:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6sch","title":"Fix btrfs_create and btrfs_mkdir to enforce EEXIST on duplicate names","description":"btrfs_create and btrfs_mkdir never check for existing entries before inserting, violating POSIX O_CREAT|O_EXCL EEXIST requirement and spec 12.1.1. Add btrfs_lookup_dir_entry check before allocation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:13.507348190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T07:35:52.412238147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T07:35:52.412214553Z","close_reason":"Added EEXIST enforcement to btrfs_create, btrfs_mkdir, and btrfs_symlink via btrfs_lookup_dir_entry check before allocation. Added 2 new tests: btrfs_write_create_duplicate_name_returns_eexist and btrfs_mkdir_duplicate_name_returns_eexist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-huh.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for ext4 write path invariants","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for ext4 write path invariants

## GOAL
Verify all invariants of the ext4 write path: extent tree mutation, block allocation, journal commit, inode updates.

## DELIVERABLES
1. Extent tree mutation tests:
   - Insert extent, split node, verify tree invariants
   - Delete extent, merge nodes, verify tree invariants
   - Truncate: remove extents beyond size
2. Block allocation tests:
   - Allocate in preferred group, verify bitmap updated
   - Allocate when preferred group full, verify fallback
   - Free blocks, verify bitmap cleared
   - Contiguous allocation preference
3. Journal commit tests:
   - Transaction bracket: begin → operations → commit
   - Rollback: begin → operations → abort → verify no changes
   - Crash simulation: commit interrupted, verify recovery
4. Inode update tests:
   - Size update after write
   - Timestamp update (mtime, ctime)
   - Link count update after link/unlink
   - Checksum recalculation

## TESTS (≥25 individual test cases)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ≥25 unit tests for ext4 write path
2. All tree/allocation invariants verified
3.  {"id":"bd-hv6","title":"Docs: Canonicalize normative type definitions (single source + cross-links)","description":"Goal: ensure every core type has ONE normative definition and every other doc reference links to it (no divergent signatures/field types).\n\nScope (must be covered):\n- ffs-types: BlockNumber, BlockSize (to add), ByteOffset (to add), InodeNumber (u64 canonical), TxnId, CommitSeq, Snapshot, GroupNumber (to add), DeviceId (to add), Generation (to add)\n- Parsing errors: ParseError and mapping boundary to FfsError\n- Any MVCC identifiers (TxnId/CommitSeq) must not fork into TxId/Lsn synonyms unless explicitly aliased.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Choose canonical source locations (prefer ffs-types + COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC glossary).\n- Replace all other duplicate definitions with 'see <canonical section>'.\n\nAcceptance:\n- rg finds no conflicting alternative definitions of these names in docs.\n- Any intentional alias is documented once (e.g. 'TxnId and TxId are the same; we standardize on TxnId').","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:12:39.148878165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:23:47.119885446Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:23:47.119866560Z","close_reason":"Canonicalized core newtype mentions across docs (removed duplicate MVCC type block in spec §1.3; updated ffs-types type lists; removed MVCC LSN synonyms in plan)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hv6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:05.300116625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ivax","title":"ffs-cli dump group command reads full ext4 image","description":"dump_group_cmd currently uses load_ext4_reader (std::fs::read whole image) even though it only needs a single ext4 group descriptor and optional raw descriptor bytes. Refactor to bounded descriptor reads from disk.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:58:02.653478319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:59:52.868804566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:59:52.868778147Z","close_reason":"Completed: dump_group_cmd now uses ext4_superblock_for_action + read_ext4_group_desc_from_path to read/parse only the target descriptor window from disk (no full-image load). Shared helper is also used by build_ext4_group_info; added regression test read_ext4_group_desc_from_path_reads_single_descriptor_window and re-verified existing group-info test. Gates: fmt, targeted tests, check, clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","memory","reliability"]}
